methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
DefSecIntel, a Series A-stage defense and security technology company founded by Jaanus Tamm, is evaluated against a peer universe of 10 private companies and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the Defense & Security sector.

The peer-median method anchors the analysis on three same-sector, same-stage private comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). The median valuation across this set is $300M, with an observed range of $150M to $500M. This method is directly applicable given stage and sector alignment and produces an implied valuation of $300M. The broader peer set includes additional established private companies such as Anduril Industries and Scale AI, though detailed financial metrics for these firms are not provided. Public market comparables in the sector (DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS) remain available for cross-validation but are not quantitatively integrated into this analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $255M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $300M. This relatively narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method with consistent peer-stage alignment. Key uncertainties include the granularity of peer financials, the degree to which public market multiples align with private growth-stage risk profiles in defense applications, and the absence of disclosed revenue or growth rate data for DefSecIntel itself. Additional revenue or comparable transaction data would strengthen conviction in this range.
